WHIPPED GOAT CHEESE DIP

This delightful appetizer hails from the Mediterranean, where goat cheese has been
a staple for centuries. The creamy, tangy richness of fresh chèvre pairs beautifully
with bright herbs and roasted garlic, creating a dip that's elegant yet approachable.
I first encountered this recipe at a small bistro in Provence, and it has since become
a favorite party appetizer in my home. The key to success is starting with the highest
quality goat cheese you can find—it truly makes all the difference.

Ingredients:
8 oz fresh goat cheese, at room temperature
4 oz cream cheese, softened
3 cloves roasted garlic, minced
2 tablespoons fresh dill, chopped
1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves
2 tablespoons fresh chives, chopped
2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice
Salt and white pepper to taste
Zest of 1 lemon
Crusty bread or crackers for serving

Method:
Combine goat cheese and cream cheese in a food processor. Blend until smooth
and creamy, about 2-3 minutes. Add roasted garlic, dill, thyme, and chives.
Pulse gently to combine without over-mixing.

Transfer to a serving bowl and drizzle with olive oil. Sprinkle with lemon
zest and season with salt and white pepper. Serve with crusty bread and
fresh vegetables.

Makes about 1 1/2 cups.
